name: 3M Quarry
short_description: Historic quarry in central New Jersey with exposure of granitic minerals. Rockhounders can find quartz, feldspar, and mica specimens in weathered pegmatite outcrops.
description:
The 3M Quarry near Hillsborough Township offers access to New Jersey's basement rock geology, where Proterozoic granitic intrusions contain pegmatitic zones rich in quartz and feldspar. The site exposes weathered granitic material typical of the New Jersey Piedmont, making it a valuable location for collectors interested in crystalline minerals and pegmatite specimens.

This quarry area sits within the Reading Prong, a series of metamorphic and granitic rocks that underlie much of northern and central New Jersey. The moderate slope and accessible terrain make it suitable for collectors of varying skill levels who want to observe granite mineralogy and collect small to medium-sized specimens. The exposure provides clear views of feldspar-rich zones and quartz veins that form through pegmatitic crystallization.

Visitors should expect typical quarry conditions with loose rock and weathered material. The site is best explored during drier months when footing is more stable. Always be aware of unstable rock faces and loose debris. Check local access before visiting, as quarry operations and private ownership may affect availability.
history: The 3M Quarry sits within the Reading Prong belt of central New Jersey, where Proterozoic-age granitic intrusions crystallized approximately 1.2 billion years ago. These granites contain pegmatitic zones where slow cooling allowed large mineral crystals to form, creating the quartz, feldspar, and mica assemblages visible at this locality today.
tips: Search along weathered granite outcrops and around the quarry edges where pegmatitic zones are most accessible. Look for quartz pockets and feldspar crystals in white to pink granitic material. Bring a rock hammer and cold chisel to carefully extract specimens from the matrix, and wear safety glasses as chips will fly when breaking apart weathered granite.
terrain_notes: Moderate slope with mixed deciduous forest cover typical of central New Jersey Piedmont. Elevation 341 feet. Quarry area exposes granitic bedrock with weathered surfaces and loose talus. Walking distance from parking approximately 0.25 to 0.5 miles depending on access point. Footing is uneven with rocky terrain and potential loose material.
written_directions: From Hillsborough Township center, take Route 206 north approximately 1.5 miles. Look for local roads leading to the quarry area near the Raritan River watershed. Exact access points vary; consult local maps or rockhounding club information for current parking and entry locations, as quarry access may be restricted by private ownership or active operations.
road_conditions: Paved roads to nearby area; final approach to quarry may be rough or blocked depending on current access
parking_notes: Limited parking near quarry access; pull-off spaces may be available along local roads. Capacity limited; verify access is permitted before visiting.
collection_rules: Access and collection are subject to private property rights and active quarry operations. Verify permission and current access status before visiting. Do not trespass on active quarry or private land. Collection of specimens may be prohibited depending on operational status and ownership.
rules:
Verify access with property owner or current operator before visiting
No trespassing on active quarry operations
Respect posted boundary markers and signs
No commercial collection or removal of large quantities
Cleaning up debris is appreciated
nearest_services: Hillsborough Township center, approximately 1.5 miles. Gas, food, and water available in town.
accessibility_notes: Moderate slope with uneven rocky terrain. No wheelchair access. Sturdy footwear and careful footing required. Loose rock and quarry conditions create ankle-twisting hazards. Not suitable for limited mobility or young children without close supervision.

faq.question: What minerals can I find at 3M Quarry in New Jersey?
faq.answer: The primary minerals found here are quartz, feldspar, and mica from granitic and pegmatitic rock exposures. Quartz occurs in veins and pockets, while feldspar crystals range from white to pink. Mica sheets are also common in the weathered granite material. This locality is excellent for studying granite mineralogy and collecting crystalline specimens.

faq.question: Is 3M Quarry open to public rockhounding and collecting?
faq.answer: Access to 3M Quarry is restricted by private ownership and active quarry operations. Before visiting, you must verify current access and obtain permission from the property owner or operator. Access may be limited or prohibited depending on operational status. Always check with local rockhounding clubs or contact the landowner directly.

faq.question: What is the best time of year to rockhound at 3M Quarry, New Jersey?
faq.answer: April through October is the ideal season, when drier conditions provide better footing on weathered granite and loose material. Winter and early spring moisture makes the quarry area slippery and potentially hazardous. Midweek visits are often safer due to reduced quarry operations.

faq.question: What tools and equipment do I need for collecting at 3M Quarry?
faq.answer: Bring a rock hammer, cold chisel, and safety glasses for extracting specimens from granite matrix. A 5-gallon bucket helps carry heavier pieces. Wear sturdy hiking boots with ankle support due to uneven, rocky terrain. Gloves protect your hands when handling sharp quarry material.

faq.question: Is 3M Quarry suitable for beginner rockhounders?
faq.answer: Yes, the site is beginner-friendly because quartz and feldspar are easy to identify and abundant in the granitic material. However, the quarry environment with loose rock, steep slopes, and potential hazards requires caution and awareness. It is best suited for collectors ages 10 and up with proper supervision and safety precautions.

faq.question: How far is 3M Quarry from Hillsborough Township, and what nearby services are available?
faq.answer: 3M Quarry is approximately 1.5 miles from Hillsborough Township center via Route 206. The township offers gas stations, restaurants, and water sources. Cell service is reliable in the area, making it safe to visit. Plan your trip with updated access information before heading out.

meta_description: Find quartz, feldspar, and mica at 3M Quarry near Hillsborough Township, NJ. Private site; verify access before collecting granite specimens.
